title: Classes - data and the code that works on it
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A class bundles data (members) with the functions that act on it (methods). public members are reachable from outside; private ones are the class's own business.
explanation: A struct grouped data; a class groups data and behaviour, and adds a wall around it. Members are the variables an object holds; methods are functions that belong to the object and work on its members. public: marks what the outside world may touch, private: keeps the internals safe so the class controls its own state. That wall - encapsulation - is the point of object-oriented C++.

exercises.question: Which keyword hides a class's internals from the outside?
